steps.action: Run 28 cross-validation tests via go test -run pattern
steps.expected: All tests pass — v2 output matches v1 via reflect.DeepEqual
steps.observed: All 28 tests passed (0.144s total). Tests cover: simple top-level, bools (8 variants), simple/nested variables, env variables (4 variants), bcrypt, convenient numbers (18 suffixes), complex samples (5), cluster config, empty/comment-only, JSON compat (4 formats), negative float, concrete types (string/bool/int64/float64/time.Time/[]any/map[string]any), duplicate key last-wins, ParseFile with simple.conf, ParseFile with multi-level includes, JSON nested blocks (3), blocks (3).

steps.action: Verify crossValidate() uses reflect.DeepEqual for v1/v2 comparison
steps.expected: Test helper compares both Parse and ParseWithChecks output
steps.observed: crossValidate() compares v1/v2 Parse output via reflect.DeepEqual AND cross-validates pedantic JSON output via bytes.Equal on json.Marshal results. crossValidateFile() compares v1/v2 ParseFile output via reflect.DeepEqual.

steps.action: Verify concrete types match: string, bool, int64, float64, time.Time, []any, map[string]any
steps.expected: Type assertions confirm all 7 concrete types
steps.observed: TestCompatConcreteTypes verifies string/bool/int64/float64, TestCompatConcreteTypeDatetime verifies time.Time, TestCompatConcreteTypeArray verifies []any, TestCompatConcreteTypeMap verifies map[string]any with int64 inner value.

steps.action: Run 5 pedantic mode tests via go test -run pattern
steps.expected: All tests pass — token wrappers have correct methods and pedantic/non-pedantic JSON match

steps.action: Verify token methods: Value(), Line(), Position(), IsUsedVariable(), SourceFile(), MarshalJSON()
steps.observed: Test verifies: Value()=int64(42), Line()=1, Position()=0, IsUsedVariable()=false, SourceFile()='', MarshalJSON()='42'. All methods present and returning correct values.

steps.action: Verify pedantic and non-pedantic produce identical JSON output
steps.observed: Test parses config with strings, ints, bools, arrays, and maps in both modes, marshals to JSON, and confirms byte-equal output.

steps.action: Verify pedantic position tracking and usedVariable marking
steps.expected: Key position and variable usage correctly tracked
steps.observed: TestCompatPedanticKeyPosition confirms Position()=4 for '    foo = 42'. TestCompatPedanticUsedVariable confirms index marked as usedVariable when referenced by $index, foo not marked.

steps.action: Verify pedantic mode with includes cross-validates against v1
steps.observed: TestCompatPedanticIncludeVariablesWithChecks creates temp dir with include files, parses with both v1 and v2 ParseFileWithChecks, confirms JSON output matches via bytes.Equal.

steps.expected: All digest tests pass — SHA-256 digests match v1 and expected values
steps.observed: All 11 tests passed (0.115s total). 10 digest sub-tests + 1 cross-validation test.

steps.action: Verify SHA-256 digest format and v1 matching
steps.expected: Digests use sha256:<hex> format and match v1 output
steps.observed: Each test compares v2 digest against expected sha256: string AND against v1 conf.ParseFileWithChecksDigest output. All match.

steps.action: Verify determinism — syntax variations produce same digest
steps.expected: Different syntax/whitespace for same values produce identical digest
steps.observed: Digests 0-3 all produce sha256:226e49e...8de despite different comment/whitespace/syntax. Digests 4-5 match despite different variable usage. Digests 6-7 match. Digests 8-9 match despite include vs inline.

steps.action: Verify usedVariable entries removed from digest computation
steps.expected: Variable definitions used as references are excluded before hashing
steps.observed: Digests 4-5 demonstrate this: config with variables (BAR_USERS=$...) and without variables produce identical digest, confirming usedVariable entries are removed before SHA-256 computation.

assertions.title: Fuzz Resilience — No Panics on Malformed Input

steps.action: Run FuzzParse seed tests (22 seeds) and TestCompatErrorOnMalformedInput
steps.expected: All seed tests pass without panics, malformed inputs return errors
steps.observed: All 22 seed tests passed. TestCompatErrorOnMalformedInput confirmed 2 malformed inputs (invalid UTF-8, truncated include) return errors.

steps.action: Run fuzz with random inputs for 15 seconds: go test -fuzz=FuzzParse -fuzztime=15s
steps.observed: 1,176,082 random inputs executed at ~89K/sec with 8 workers over 15 seconds. 0 panics. 109 new interesting coverage paths discovered (644 total). PASS.

steps.action: Verify fuzz target exercises both Parse and ParseWithChecks
steps.expected: Both functions called on every input
steps.observed: FuzzParse calls Parse(data) and ParseWithChecks(data) on every input, ensuring both code paths are fuzz-tested.

steps.action: Verify seed corpus covers edge cases: malformed input, invalid UTF-8, truncated constructs, integer overflow
steps.observed: Seeds include: empty string, whitespace-only, invalid UTF-8 (A@@Føøøø?˛ø), truncated include, integer overflow (99999999999999999999), block strings, variable references, bcrypt, datetime, IP:port.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: all 4 passed. VAL-PARSER-023: 28 cross-validation tests confirm Parse/ParseFile produce identical map[string]any output to v1 via reflect.DeepEqual across all value types. VAL-PARSER-024: 5 tests confirm pedantic token wrappers implement all required methods and pedantic/non-pedantic JSON output matches. VAL-PARSER-025: 11 tests confirm SHA-256 digest computation matches v1, is deterministic across syntax variations, and correctly removes usedVariable entries. VAL-PARSER-026: 22 seed tests + 1,176,082 random fuzz inputs confirm no panics on any input.
